rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for the Court to determine why no petition has been filed to close the estate, as ordered. Please review the 6th amended general order on the court's website re the strong preference for all attorneys and parties to appear remotely as a result of the COVID-19 emergency and for information on appearing by CourtCall or via Zoom. (A 7th amended general order is imminent. Parties should check the Court's website closer to the hearing date for further instructions regarding remote appearance.) For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILLIAM H. QUAYLE TRUST DATED NOV. 11, 1980
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #6, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Parties are to update Court on their settlement discussions. Court is to determine how to proceed. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 6th Amended General Order which took effect on 07/21/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JULIA CASEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance highly encouraged. Please review the 6th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their full name. ISSUES: 1) Matter set for hearing because the attorney Declaration not signed or verified by petitioner does not adequately address issue 1a. Examiner sent the following notes: a. Petitions need to include sufficient allegations to allow the Court to determine administration is proceeding properly. Please make all necessary allegations as if the petition was for final distribution, including but not limited to: Notice under Probate Code 9202; Creditor's Claims; Assignments/Powers of Attorney; Requests for Special Notice, etc. b.A hard copy of the proposed order must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. (LRSF 14.93). [Proposed orders may not be e-filed.]

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: LILY GEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues may include: 1) Objection has been filed by Terry Gee. 2) Petitioner requests instructions to distribute real property located at 44th Ave to the intended beneficiary of the Trust. No allegation is made as to why an order is necessary. 3) Petitioner requests order directing the beneficiary of the real property at 44th Ave to execute any documents necessary to effectuate the transfer of property. Petitioner does not specify what documents would be needed and why this order is needed. 4) Petitioner requests instruction directing her to distribute $775,000 from the trust to Terry Gee representing a buyout for her interest in real property located at 45th Avenue. 5) Petitioner requests a finding that the Trust is not responsible for seismic retrofit expenses for real property specifically gifted and that any unpaid expenses paid by the Trust should be allocated to the beneficiary's share of the estate. 6) Petitioner requests approval of distributions made and proposed distributions in accordance with the Third Amendment and per judgment of Trustee. Court to determine whether surviving settlor had the ability to amend the trust upon death of deceased settlor. 7) Petitioner requests approval of accountings that contain distributions pursuant to the third trust amendment. 8) Petitioner requests approval of compensation of 1% of the fair market value. Timesheets have not been provided. 9) Two accountings have been filed, the Survivor's Trust and the Marital Trust, in the same petition. 10) Tri Counties Bank statement ending 5583 filed on 8/13/20 shows the account being titled in the name of Trustee, as an individual, and not as the trustee of this trust. In addition, the balance shown as of the end of account date ($82,388) does not match what is provided in the Survivor's Trust accounting ($69,388.56). Remote appearances are highly encouraged. Please review the 6th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 6th amended general order. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FIRST AND LAST NAME.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/30/2020 (no earlier date available). Notes were e-mailed to counsel on 10/26/2020.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILTSEK 1989 REVOCABLE TRUST DATED AUGUST 16, 1989
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition Confirming Property As Assets Of The Trust And Instructing Co-Trustees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/9/20. No courtesy copies. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.93 G.] A cursory glance at the petition online did not indicate that beneficiaries were listed, as required by CRC 7.902. The incorrect form of Notice of Hearing was served. Additionally, if there are any heirs who are not trust beneficiaries, notice must be personally served (or waived). [Probate Code Section 17203(b) and CCP Section 413.10 et seq.] A copy of the statement from the financial institution dated immediately preceding the date of death must be filed to confirm how title to the account was held. A copy of the supplement must also be served on all interested parties. [CRC 7.53(b).] A courtesy copy of the petition, verified supplement thereto, and proof(s) of service must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 11/30/20.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ZETHER MCGRIGER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/30/20. A fully amended petition was filed, which requires the same notice as the original petition, including publication. Notice of Petition to Administer Estate and Proof of Publication on amended petition must be filed, which both must indicate that an AMENDED petition has been filed. A proposed order also has not been submitted. All required documents to be provided to Room 202 at least two weeks prior.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF THOMAS ELKING WESTERBERG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/9/20. (No earlier availability.) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.26 D. and E.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? Item 6d should not be checked when another box is checked above it. A supplement to the petition, prepared on pleading paper and signed and verified by the petitioner, must be filed to address these issues. A courtesy copy of the supplement must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 11/30/20.
